title: cracca uniovulata
intro: Cracca uniovulata, also known as Hedyotis uniovulata, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various respiratory issues such as cough and cold. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction for medicinal use, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal preparations.
